articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates significant improvements in long-horizon planning, mathematical proof generation, and agentic tool use, narrowing the gap on several AGI benchmarks. Enterprise API access is rolling out with new structured-output guarantees and latency optimizations.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Ultra 2 Achieves State-of-the-Art on Scientific Reasoning Benchmarks
articles.summary: DeepMind published results for Gemini Ultra 2, which sets new records on GPQA-Diamond, SWE-Bench Verified, and a newly introduced materials-science reasoning benchmark. The model leverages a sparse mixture-of-experts architecture with improved routing and trains on a curated corpus of peer-reviewed scientific literature. Researchers highlight its ability to propose novel hypotheses in protein-ligand binding affinity prediction.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Demonstrates Fully Autonomous Warehouse Shift with Figure 03 Humanoids
articles.summary: Figure Robotics ran a 10-hour fully autonomous warehouse logistics shift using a fleet of 20 Figure 03 humanoid robots at a BMW facility in South Carolina, handling pick-and-place, palletizing, and inter-station transport without human intervention. The robots coordinated via a centralized planner built on a vision-language-action model fine-tuned for manipulation tasks. The demonstration marks a milestone in commercial viability for general-purpose humanoid labor.

articles.title: Tesla Optimus Gen 3 Enters Limited Production, First Units Deployed Internally
articles.summary: Tesla confirmed that Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robots have entered low-rate initial production at its Gigafactory Texas, with the first batch deployed on Tesla's own assembly lines for component sorting and quality inspection. The new generation features 28 degrees of freedom in each hand, improved bipedal locomotion over uneven terrain, and an on-board inference chip running a distilled version of Tesla's FSD vision transformer stack. Musk stated the goal is external sales beginning Q4 2026.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude 'Opus 4' with Real-Time Tool Use and Persistent Memory
articles.summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4, featuring a persistent conversational memory layer that maintains structured context across sessions and a revamped tool-use framework allowing real-time interaction with external APIs, databases, and code sandboxes. Benchmarks show substantial gains in multi-step agentic coding tasks, with Opus 4 completing 74% of SWE-Bench Verified issues end-to-end. The release intensifies competition at the frontier model tier as enterprises evaluate autonomous AI coding agents.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Risk Production of 1.4nm Process Node Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: TSMC announced that its A14 (1.4nm-class) process node has entered risk production at its Fab 20 facility in Hsinchu, roughly one quarter ahead of the originally communicated timeline. The node uses gate-all-around nanosheet transistors with backside power delivery, promising an 18% speed improvement and 30% power reduction versus N2. Early customers reportedly include Apple and NVIDIA for next-generation mobile and data center AI accelerator chips.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface Implant
articles.summary: The FDA granted de novo clearance to Neuralink's N2 implant, a second-generation brain-computer interface featuring 4,096 electrodes—4x the original—along with bidirectional stimulation capability and wireless charging. Clinical trial data from the PRIME2 study showed paralyzed participants achieving cursor control speeds approaching able-bodied benchmarks. The clearance allows expanded trials with up to 50 new participants and brings the device closer to broader therapeutic use in spinal cord injury and ALS patients.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Hyundai Unveil Atlas EV: An Electric Commercial Humanoid for Construction
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics, backed by Hyundai, publicly demonstrated Atlas EV, a fully electric commercial humanoid robot purpose-built for construction environments. The robot can carry 50 kg payloads, navigate scaffolding, and perform overhead drilling tasks using a combination of LiDAR SLAM and a vision-language planner for instruction following. Pilot programs with three major construction firms are set to begin in summer 2026, signaling a serious push into an industry plagued by labor shortages.
